hero.title: Install OpenClaw
hero.description: Check the environment, install OpenClaw with the official bootstrapper, fill in the API Key, and finally start Gateway. This page turns the onboarding path into one clear flow.

fixRuntime.title: Fix the runtime first
fixRuntime.description: This is not a desktop environment that can run the local installer. Switch to the desktop runtime first, then continue.

ready.title: You are ready to go
ready.description: OpenClaw is installed and running. Open the Dashboard now.

service.title: Next step: start Gateway
service.description: The API Key is saved. Start Gateway now and you can begin using the app.

config.title: Installation complete
config.description: The next thing to do is set the API Key and save it.

env.title: Refresh environment
env.description: The official installer can bootstrap missing Node.js / npm automatically, but you can still refresh the environment at any time.

start.title: Start installation
start.description: Open the wizard and follow the prompts to finish the OpenClaw install.

order.title: Install order
order.description: Install OpenClaw first, then fill in the API Key in Config, save it, and start Gateway in Service.

runbook.title: Current step
runbook.description: Focus on one step at a time. Do not try to handle install, config, and service together.

environment.title: Installation prep
environment.description: Check the local environment and OpenClaw detection state before starting installation.

status.previewMode: You are in browser preview mode, so installation is disabled. Use the ClawDesk desktop app or `npm run tauri:dev` to install locally.
status.runtimeUnavailable: The desktop command bridge is unavailable, so installation is disabled. Fix the Tauri runtime integration first.
status.npmMissing: Node.js / npm is not detected, but the official installer can bootstrap them automatically.
status.bootstrapHint: The official installer will bootstrap missing Node.js / npm automatically, so you can continue.
status.installHint: Click "Install OpenClaw" and the install will run in the background. The result will appear when it finishes.

result.hintSuccess: OpenClaw is ready. Fill in the API Key next, then you can start the service.
result.hintWarning: Core components were installed successfully. Fill in the API Key first, then continue the remaining steps on the Service page.
result.hintFailure: Something went wrong during installation. Check the logs, fix the issue, and run the install again.

wizard.title: OpenClaw installation wizard
wizard.nowOnly: Only this step needs to be completed now: {{step}}
wizard.nextStep: After this step, the next one is: {{step}}

sequence.title: Follow this order
sequence.description: This is not a diagnostics page. It only keeps the install path needed by new users.

after.title: What to do after install
after.description: 1. Save the config and fill in the API Key. 2. Start Gateway. 3. Open the Dashboard to begin using the app.

platform.title: Look at the right system card
platform.description: The three platforms share one install flow, but commands, permissions, and troubleshooting priorities differ.

platform.title: Platform Guidance
platform.description: Windows, macOS, and Linux share the same product flow, but command paths, permission models, and troubleshooting focus differ.

pending.detail: Waiting to check Node.js, npm, and local install prerequisites.
pending.suggestion: Refresh the environment first to confirm Node.js and npm visibility.

pending.detail: Waiting to run the OpenClaw CLI install.
pending.suggestion: The install command will call the official bootstrapper and fill in missing Node.js if needed.

pending.detail: Waiting to run the Gateway managed install.
pending.suggestion: After the CLI install finishes, it will continue to install the Gateway service.

pending.detail: Waiting to confirm the executable path and next steps.
pending.suggestion: After the install completes, the executable path will be verified and next steps shown.
